name: chargeback
description: Chargeback lifecycle tracker. Answers 'has this payment been forcibly reversed by the customer via their bank, and how are we defending against it?'. Ships TWO tables: `chargebacks` (root record — one per chargeback filed by the cardholder's bank; escalated from `PaymentDispute` when the tenant loses the pre-chargeback dispute; carries reason_code + network + amount + status + evidence_due_by + provider_reference), `chargeback_evidence` (per-chargeback evidence submission — invoice + receipt + service_documentation + refund_policy + communication_log + shipping_docs + customer_signature_evidence + uncategorized_text; can have multiple submission attempts with the last one winning). Chargeback state machine: `pending_evidence` -> `evidence_submitted` -> `under_bank_review` -> `won` | `lost` | `expired` (evidence deadline passed with no submission) | `accepted_refund` (RDR path — auto-refund before chargeback filed). When `won`: parent Payment.amount_disputed_cents reduced back; fires ChargebackWon. When `lost`: money permanently gone; records offsetting Transaction (debit chargebacks_expense, credit cash); records penalty fee via secondary Transaction (debit chargeback_fees, credit cash — network fee $15-25 per chargeback); fires marketing::ChargebackFiled (negative-value + fraud signal); cascades referrals::ClawbackReward when applicable. IMMUTABLE after terminal status. Card network reason codes catalogued (Visa 10.xx / 11.xx / 12.xx / 13.xx; Mastercard 4837 / 4863 / 4870 / 4834 / 4841 / 4842 / 4853 / 4855; Amex F14 / F24 / F29 / P01 / P05; Discover 4842 / 4855). Rapid Dispute Resolution (RDR) integration for Visa — auto-refund below threshold to close pre-chargeback state. Compelling Evidence 3.0 (CE3.0) support for Visa merchants.
planned_consumers: growth::marketing (Wave 5 — every ChargebackLost fires a marketing::ChargebackFiled negative-value conversion event; ad networks subtract from ROAS + retrain fraud models on the click IDs behind chargeback-generating purchases)
growth::referrals (Wave 5 — chargeback on an invoice with a materialized referral reward cascades a ClawbackReward event; the referrals module reverses the reward + notifies the referrer)

packages: owen-it/laravel-auditing ^13.x (compliance-grade audit trail — every write to chargebacks/chargeback_evidence emits an audit row with 7-year retention; 10y Enterprise)
wraps.rationale: Provider-side webhook verification flows through payment::PaymentWebhookVerifier — this module does NOT own signature-verification code. Evidence collection delegates to invoice + membership + notifications modules via EvidenceCollector — this module does NOT own the primary sources. Refund creation on RDR path delegates to refund::RefundOrchestrator. Transaction recording (offsetting entry + network fee) delegates to transaction::TransactionRecorder. laravel-auditing is the codebase-standard audit trait — every finance table uses it (see invoice + payment + transaction + tax + refund modules).

tenant.description: Tenant-facing chargeback surface. Admin+finance routes only — customers NEVER interact with chargebacks over the tenant HTTP surface (chargebacks are bank-initiated; customers file with their bank, not with us). Admin+finance can list chargebacks, view detail + evidence, submit evidence, accept a chargeback via RDR (auto-refund path), mark a chargeback lost manually after ops investigation, and read the chargeback rate report. Every route carries `sanctum` guard + `X-Application-Id` + `entitlement.enforce:chargeback_management`. Evidence submission additionally carries `entitlement.enforce:chargeback_evidence_submission`.

platform-admin.description: Cross-tenant support tooling. Ops + support review chargeback status across tenants for escalation cases (repeat-offender tenants approaching VDMP/MDMP thresholds, drift reconciliation), audit financial reversal records, and access chargeback evidence for compliance investigations. Redacted view — customer PII is hashed unless the caller holds platform.chargeback.pii.view. The rate-threshold-alerts endpoint surfaces cross-tenant chargeback-rate alerts for merchant-account risk monitoring — Academorix ops must intervene when any tenant approaches 0.9% (Visa) or 1.5% (Mastercard) rate thresholds.
